mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth remains above 2% annually, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production below -0.5% reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced factory output, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns dominate when INDPRO contracts, or the signal reverses direction during periods of stable CPI growth.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 5.0%, real rates turn negative, prompting equity market repricing toward growth assets and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The positive return signal disappears when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%, or Rank IC for the interaction term falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue projections, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive quarterly returns when unemployment is above 5.5%, or the signal produces negative IC over 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below 0.0% chained dollars signals an economic recession, which immediately reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in >55% of quarters following negative GDP growth, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation indicates an overheating economy where supply constraints outpace demand, forcing central banks to tighten and causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when both series rise but FEDFUNDS is cut, or 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered cases.
